The system was out for just over 2 years, and if you played any early nintendo games, this was impressive. Yeah, the NES had graphically better games later in its life, but all consoles do as development goes on and developers better use the hardware. Look at mario 1 and then mario 3. Same thing. Time goes on, graphics improve. It was damn good for such an early game

text:
So people knew to write in to Nintendo Power Magazine when the first issue hadn't been published yet and thus, there was no address to write to? That's some pretty infallible logic you have there. [/sarcasm]

author: @QuiGonZel

text:
Nintendo did not steal this song from a kid who wrote in to Nintendo Power. 

Nintendo Power's first issue wasn't published until July of 1988. The Legend of Zelda was released in North America on August of 1987. So no, they did not steal it. The kid stole it from the commercial.
